name: Kun Wang
summary: I am a postdoctoral researcher at the Institute of Microstructure Technology, Karlsruhe Institute of Technology. My research focuses on sustainable thermal management, elastocaloric heat pumps, soft active regenerators, and numerical design for scalable solid-state cooling systems.

facts.label: Current position
facts.value: Postdoctoral Researcher, Institute of Microstructure Technology, KIT

facts.label: Research focus
facts.value: Elastocaloric cooling, regenerative heat pumps, magnetocaloric materials, additive manufacturing

facts.label: Education
facts.value: Ph.D., Technical University of Denmark; M.S., University of Chinese Academy of Sciences

research.title: Sustainable Thermal Management
research.description: I work on zero-emission cooling and heat-pump technologies that connect materials science, mechanical design, and thermal system modeling.

research.title: Soft Elastocaloric Devices
research.description: My current research explores low-cost elastocaloric systems, including natural-rubber-based concepts, soft active regenerators, and 3D-printed device architectures.

research.title: Modeling And Scalable Design
research.description: I develop numerical and thermomechanical models for active regenerators, woven porous structures, and solid-state cooling devices that can move toward practical applications.

publications.title: Numerical study of enhanced cooling in active elastocaloric regenerators with porous woven structures
publications.authors: K. Wang, M. Kohl, J. Xu

publications.title: A 3D-printed full-soft regenerative elastocaloric cooler
publications.authors: K. Wang, K. Engelbrecht, C. R. H. Bahl, R. Bjork

publications.title: Volume compensation of large-deformation 3D-printed soft elastomeric elastocaloric regenerators
publications.authors: K. Wang, J. T. B. Overvelde, K. Engelbrecht, R. Bjork, C. R. H. Bahl

publications.title: Additive manufactured thermoplastic elastomers for low-stress driven elastocaloric cooling
publications.authors: K. Wang, K. Engelbrecht, C. R. H. Bahl

publications.title: High-throughput characterization of the adiabatic temperature change for magnetocaloric materials
publications.authors: K. Wang, Y. Ouyang, Y. Shen, Y. Zhang, M. Zhang, J. Liu

publications.title: Microchannel magnetic regenerators with optimized porosity by electrodischarge drilling
publications.authors: L. Miao, K. Wang, X. Lu, Y. Zhang, J. Liu
